meta.feed_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

items.title: OKC Thunder bury LA Lakers to take 3-0 NBA Playoff series lead
items.summary: Thunder use an explosive second half to beat LeBron James and the Los Angeles Lakers 131-108 as Ajay Mitchell scores 24.

items.title: Syria sees first government reshuffle since al-Assad’s ouster: State media
items.summary: Interim President Ahmed al-Sharaa's reshuffle comes amid protests over poor government performance.

items.title: Rebel fighters kill at least 69 people in northeastern DR Congo
items.summary: Security officials say CODECO militia groups carried out the attack in Ituri province.

items.title: JNIM and Tuareg fighters join forces
items.summary: AJ's Defence Editor breaks down the latest threat to Mali’s government and the rapid retreat of Russian forces there

items.title: Deadly car bomb attack hits checkpoint in northwest Pakistan
items.summary: A suspected car bombing followed by a firefight at a police checkpoint has killed more than a dozen people in Bannu.

items.title: Iran war day 72: Tehran yet to reply to US plan; Israel bombs Lebanon
items.summary: Israel kills at least 24 people in Lebanon in one day as Tehran's response to US proposal to end the war is awaited.

items.title: Flotilla activists arrive in Turkiye before setting sail to Gaza
items.summary: Over 30 Global Sumud Flotilla vessels have reached Marmaris on Turkiye’s coast, preparing for their mission to Gaza.

items.title: Flotilla activist gives defiant message after Israel deports him to Greece
items.summary: After a week in Israeli custody, flotilla activist Saif Abu Keshek has been deported to Greece.

items.title: Sabalenka out of Italian Open amid injury concerns for Roland Garros
items.summary: ⁠Sorana Cirstea defeated world's top women's player 2-6, 6-3, 7-5 in the third round as she suffered pain in lower back.

items.title: Israel deports two Gaza aid flotilla activists
items.summary: The pair was abducted by Israeli forces from the aid flotilla in late April and held in Israel.

items.title: Cruise ship hit by hantavirus outbreak arrives in Tenerife
items.summary: Hantavirus is usually spread by rodents but can in rare cases be transmitted person to person.

items.title: Strickland delivers first defeat to Chimaev on split decision at UFC 328
items.summary: Sean Strickland won 48–47, 48–47 and 47–48 against Khamzat Chimaev in a fight that failed to live up to its hype.

items.title: Dubois stops Wardley in 11th round to take WBO heavyweight boxing title
items.summary: Daniel Dubois recovered from two knockdowns to win a second World Heavyweight title with a stoppage of Fabio Wardley.

items.title: Macron tours East Africa amid push to redefine France’s role in Africa
items.summary: Paris seeks to repair economic and security ties while countering rising anti-French sentiment across Africa.

items.title: Two wins, two losses: What India, Pakistan have learned a year after war
items.summary: India and Pakistan each claim strategic successes after the four-day conflict, as tensions continue to simmer.

items.title: Person dies after being hit by plane at Denver airport
items.summary: A Frontier Airlines plane has hit and killed a person at Denver’s international airport.

items.title: Putin suggests Russia’s war on Ukraine ‘coming to an end’
items.summary: Russian president also says he is willing to meet Zelenskyy in a third country if a long-term peace deal is reached.

items.title: ‘No Kings’ protest outside Buckingham Palace
items.summary: British anti-royals have staged a ‘No Kings’ protest outside Buckingham Palace in London.

items.title: Satellite images show likely oil slick off Iran’s Kharg Island
items.summary: Satellite images have captured a suspected oil slick spanning dozens of square kilometres near Iran’s Kharg Island.

items.title: Iran war live: IRGC warns US against attacks on ships; Israel bombs Lebanon
items.summary: US-Iran ceasefire holds as Tehran warns Washington against attacks on tankers and Israel kills 24 people in Lebanon.

items.title: WHO chief says Hantavirus outbreak ‘is not COVID’
items.summary: WHO chief says Hantavirus outbreak ‘is not COVID’

items.title: Brazil judge bars law that could reduce Bolsonaro’s 27-year prison sentence
items.summary: Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes suspends use of law to reduce prison sentences, pending further review.

items.title: One killed in Gaza as Turkish FM talks peace efforts with Hamas official
items.summary: Israel continues to violate a 'ceasefire' agreed to in October with hundreds more killed across the Strip.

items.title: For the first time, Putin says he’s open to meeting Zelensky outside Russia
items.summary: Vladimir Putin changes tone, says for first time he will meet Zelenskyy in a third nation.

items.title: Palestinian family says settlers forced them to exhume father’s body
items.summary: A Palestinian family in Jenin says Israeli settlers forced them to exhume their father hours after his funeral.
